title: When They Said They Were Not Looking for Anything Serious
subtitle: A field guide to mixed signals and what people actually mean.
summary: From soft launches to slow fades, here is what readers asked about most this week in modern dating.
brandExplainer: The Kiss and Tell is a weekly letter on dating, relationships, and the stories we do not forget.

entries.title: The Text That Changed the Whole Vibe
entries.body: You thought you were on the same page until one message landed sideways. We unpack what usually sits underneath the casual tone.

entries.title: Three Kinds of Almost-Relationships
entries.body: Situationship, slow burn, or just bad timing? A quick taxonomy so you can name what you are in without spiraling.

entries.title: What Friends Get Wrong
entries.body: Your group chat means well, but the advice that works for one person can wreck another. Here is a gentler playbook.

entries.title: Green Flags Worth More Than Chemistry
entries.body: Consistency beats fireworks. Readers shared the small behaviors that made them feel safe enough to stay.

entries.title: Poll: Who Should Text First After a Great Date?
entries.body: The results were closer than you would expect—and the comments were better than the poll.

entries.title: What We Are Watching Next Week
entries.body: A new column on boundaries without the therapy-speak. Send your questions—we might answer one anonymously.

disclaimer: Disclaimer: All names and identifiable details have been modified to protect the reputations of our contributors in the eyes of their partners, colleagues, and parents.
